Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search contacts for Robert Calloway using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Robert" or "Calloway") to find his email address (r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com) and contact ID (staff_e85bc913c756). This is needed before searching his emails and before sending the final email to him.
OE 2: Search contacts for Denise Holloway using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Denise" or "Holloway") to find her email address (denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com). The prompt names Denise, so the agent needs to identify who she is.
OE 3: Search contacts for Priya Chakrabarti using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Priya" or "Chakrabarti") to confirm her email address (priya.chakrabarti@keystonemortgage.com) for use as the sender in the final email to Robert.
OE 4: Search emails for Robert's incoming mail using search_emails (query: "robert.calloway" or "r.calloway" or "Robert") to discover the confidential matters landing in his inbox. Note: Robert's email appears in two formats across the data -- r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (in contacts) and robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (in many email threads) -- so the agent should search broadly to get complete coverage. Key emails the agent should find include: Grace's notification about Brittany Wallace's formal harassment complaint against Jordan Blake (with the note about Robert's "personal closeness" with Jordan), Grace's documented performance concerns about Amy Chen, Darnell Price's confidential complaint about Grace Yamamoto's management conduct, Sofia Reyes's separate confidential complaint about Grace, Denise's CFPB fair lending review plan (15-day clock), Denise's email to outside counsel Laura Bennett about Marcus Webb's data exfiltration and non-compete, and Grace's recommendation for Carlos Rivera's PIP.
OE 5: Search emails with additional queries related to specific matters using search_emails (queries such as "complaint", "confidential", "harassment", "Marcus Webb", "departure", "performance", "CFPB", "Keith", "Glassdoor", "Thompson" or similar) to discover additional confidential streams beyond the initial search. Key additional matters include: Keith Langford's age discrimination complaint referencing "younger energy" language from a Slack exchange between Robert and Grace, the Thompson file CD timing discrepancy where Grace could not explain the date, Glassdoor reviews describing internal culture concerns (favoritism, compensation, management friction), the Natasha Okafor borrower coaching issue, and the Keisha Williams retaliation complaint about her comp tier being reduced after she challenged compliance audit findings.
OE 6: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (queries such as "confidential", "complaint", "Jordan", "Brittany", "Amy", "Marcus", "CFPB", "Keisha", "Keith", "younger energy", "Glassdoor" or similar) to find internal discussions about these confidential matters. Key Slack artifacts include: Jordan Blake's public comments about Brittany Wallace in #general ("front desk has a whole different vibe today"), Natasha Okafor's private DM to Grace about Amy Chen (channel D_GRACE_NATASHA -- "something may be going on with her beyond normal burnout", mentioning a surgery), Denise's fair lending review posts in #compliance-alerts (C004) including the preservation notice and CFPB response coordination, Keisha Williams's pushback on the CFPB complaint framing in #loan-processing (C002), and any internal discussions about the various investigations.
OE 7: Search Slack DM channels using conversations_history on DM channels (such as D_GRACE_NATASHA, D_grace_robert, D_denise_grace, or other DM channels found via channels_list) to discover private conversations about the confidential matters. The Natasha-Grace DM about Amy's suspected post-surgery health issue is particularly important because it represents medical information that should not cross into the performance review stream. The Grace-Robert DM channel (D_grace_robert) contains critical evidence including the "younger energy" language cited in Keith's age complaint and Robert's instruction to keep Keisha in the lower comp tier.
OE 8: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements or crm_search_contacts to find engagement notes related to the confidential matters. CRM engagement notes may contain documentation about the harassment complaint, performance reviews, or compliance investigations that provide additional participant information.
OE 9: Search the filesystem using filesystem_show_data to discover any documents related to the confidential matters, such as Brittany Wallace's written statement PDF (referenced as saved for preservation), QC audit findings, incident reports, or HR documentation. These filesystem artifacts would show additional people who have access to confidential information.
OE 10: Look up the LOS staff list using mortgage_los_list_staff to identify all Keystone employees and their roles. This helps map who could have access to confidential matters through their system roles and helps the agent understand the organizational relationships (e.g., Grace as Director of Ops leading investigations, Denise as Compliance Officer, Jordan as BD Manager).
OE 11: Write a confidential memo to the filesystem using filesystem_write_file (or filesystem_create_directory followed by a file write). The memo should be saved to a private path (such as "confidential/robert-calloway-matter-overlap-memo-2026-04-28.txt" or similar). The memo content must cover: (a) each distinct confidential matter identified, (b) who knows about each matter, (c) cross-matter conflicts flagged -- specifically that Grace Yamamoto is leading/handling investigations in multiple matters while being the subject of confidential complaints from Darnell Price and Sofia Reyes about her own management conduct, that Jordan Blake is the subject of the harassment complaint and Robert's close personal friend, that Denise Holloway is involved in every matter with potential fact-witness conflicts, and (d) recommended actions.
OE 12: Send an email using send_email from priya.chakrabarti@keystonemortgage.com to r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (or robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com). The email subject should reference the confidential nature and the upcoming meetings. The email body should summarize the findings from the memo, highlight the most critical conflicts (Grace's dual role as investigator and investigated, Jordan/Robert personal closeness, Denise's overextension), reference the saved memo location, and note this is for Robert's eyes only before his meetings this week.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

The email summary also references this.

5 The filesystem memo content or email body identifies a performance concern about Amy Chen.
outcome

This directly identifies the performance concern about an LO (Amy Chen).
✗Run #4Neither the filesystem memo nor the email body mentions Amy Chen's performance concern. Amy Chen is only briefly mentioned in Matter 3 as someone who knew about Danielle Webb on loan files, not as the subject of a performance review.
✗Run #5The memo identifies Carlos Rivera as the LO performance concern (Matter 3: PIP), not Amy Chen. Amy Chen appears only briefly as someone 'aware of some file-level discussions' in the Marcus Webb section. The agent failed to discover or report Amy Chen's performance concern.
